item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A stays broadly supportive of society-priority, but frames it as social justice, public needs, and minority inclusion rather than unlimited group dominance. Agent B remains opposed to group-dominance and emphasizes individual/minority protections, but moves from a clearer individual-rights position toward a more neutral critique of majority rule and institutional safeguards by the final turn. Agent A's opening contains all-caps and awkward Indonesian, but the later turns are readable.
